summary: Distinctive Late Jurassic carnivore identified by its nasal horn and paired brow horns. InGen's Ceratosaurus stock was monitored for territorial aggression in mixed-predator sectors.
facts: Prominent nasal horn and paired horns above the eyes — key field identifiers.
Row of osteoderms along the midline suggests armored display or defense.
Hunts along forest margins and riparian corridors.
Cryo embryo shows stable theropod marker integrity.
fieldNotes: Maintain distance. Specimen may charge if cornered or if nasal horn display is triggered.
